div1.entryFree:
Lard, 1) to fatten: Falstaff sweats to death and --s the lean earth, H4A II, 2, 116. it is the pasture --s the brother's sides, Tim. IV, 3, 12. cf. Enlard.
2) to enrich, to garnish: the mirth so --ed with my matter, Wiv. IV, 6, 14. in which array, brave soldier, doth he lie, --ing the plain, H5 IV, 6, 8. wit --ed with malice, Troil. V, 1, 63. --ed with sweet flowers, Hml. IV, 5, 37. an exact command, --ed with many several sorts of reasons, V, 2, 20.
